criteria.label: State Minkowski distance and identify Manhattan (p=1) and Euclidean (p=2) as special cases.
criteria.example: Write d_p(u,v) = (Σ|uᵢ−vᵢ|^p)^(1/p) and compute p=1 → sum of abs gaps, p=2 → sqrt of sum of squared gaps for u=(0,0), v=(3,4) giving 7 and 5.

criteria.label: Explain when cosine similarity is preferable to Euclidean distance and give a counterexample where they disagree.
criteria.example: Argue cosine for embedding retrieval when length is uninformative; show (1,2) vs (10,20) have cosine 1.0 but positive L2 distance.

criteria.label: Configure scikit-learn k-NN with scaling and compare at least two metrics on a tiny dataset.
criteria.example: Build `make_pipeline(StandardScaler(), KNeighborsClassifier(metric='minkowski', p=1))` vs p=2 on 3-row height/income data and show predictions can differ for the same query.

criteria.label: Match distance metrics to algorithm assumptions (k-NN vs k-means vs RBF kernel) and justify preprocessing choices.
criteria.example: State that k-means assumes Euclidean on scaled features, k-NN accepts Minkowski/cosine, RBF SVM uses exp(−γ||u−v||²); recommend z-scoring tabular features before Euclidean k-NN.
